title: grevillea sericea var. diffusa
intro: Grevillea sericea var. diffusa, also known as the silvery oil flower, is a shrub native to Australia with silvery-green foliage and white or cream-colored flowers. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from the oil found in the leaves. The health benefits of Grevillea sericea var. diffusa include its potential to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and aid in wound healing. Grevillea sericea var. diffusa can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, salves, and creams, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
